summary: DHH recounts the frustrating experience of trying to take his own photo, requiring 57 attempts before getting a single acceptable shot. He describes the difficulty of composing a flattering expression for a fraction of a second, the challenges of holding a camera at arm's length, and even enlisting a mirror to watch himself while shooting. The post is a lighthearted, self-deprecating slice-of-life entry from the early days of his blog.
key_points: Taking a self-portrait required 57 shots to produce one remotely acceptable photo
The challenge of controlling your facial expression for a fraction-of-a-second exposure is surprisingly difficult
Holding a camera at arm's length made framing nearly impossible, with many shots missing his face entirely
He used a mirror to watch himself while releasing the shutter, still with poor results
money_quotes: It took 57 shots before I had one I even remotely liked. 57.
I was still stunned on how hard it is to shape your face desirably for that 1/80 of a second of exposure.
Some of those lesser fortunate 57 shots were just... awful. I can't believe they depicted the same person as is displayed on that one decent shot.
Another chunk had me looking incredibly, well, evil. It's hard to smile at yourself and a camera.
And no, you can not see those 56 mishaps. Vainity, you know.
key_insight: Before the era of smartphones and selfie cameras, taking your own photo was a comically frustrating exercise in trial and error.
